risks: No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Log Analytics, Azure Monitor) — operational blind spot for pipeline failures and performance issues
Single-region deployment (eastus2) with Standard_LRS storage — no disaster recovery or cross-region redundancy
All resources have publicNetworkAccess set to Enabled with no VNet integration or private endpoints — data in transit between services traverses the public internet
Databricks enableNoPublicIp is false — cluster worker nodes receive public IP addresses, increasing attack surface
No explicit RBAC role assignment resources defined — managed identity role grants (e.g., Storage Blob Data Contributor) are described in reasoning but not modeled as plan resources
Synapse SQL administrator password handling is implicit — no mechanism in the plan ensures the password is sourced from Key Vault at deployment time
correctionsRecommended: Add a Log Analytics workspace resource and configure diagnostic settings for ADF, Databricks, Synapse, Storage, and Key Vault
Add Azure Monitor alert rules for pipeline failures, long-running activities, and Synapse SQL pool DTU usage
Consider upgrading storage redundancy to Standard_GRS or Standard_ZRS for production resilience
Add VNet integration and private endpoints for Storage, Key Vault, Synapse, and ADF to enforce network isolation
Set Databricks enableNoPublicIp to true to prevent public IP allocation on cluster nodes
Add explicit RBAC role assignment resources (Storage Blob Data Contributor for ADF and Synapse managed identities, Key Vault Secrets User for ADF)
Add a resource group resource or specify the target resource group in the plan for deployment clarity
Consider adding resource tags (environment, cost-center, project) for operational governance
